updatedJobs.title: EFG Graduate Programme 2026 - Corporate Functions - Human Resources - Lugano

summary:
We are looking for a young talent to join our EFG Graduate Programme – Corporate Functions path in Lugano, with a focus on Human Resources.

In this role, you will gain hands-on experience and valuable insight into the key areas that support and drive the success of EFG.

You will have the opportunity to work closely with our HR team, participate in talent acquisition, employee development, and performance management initiatives, and contribute to projects that shape EFG’s people strategy. This experience will provide you with a comprehensive understanding of how Human Resources enables EFG to deliver outstanding service and maintain its leadership in private banking.

How the EFG Graduate Programme works?

Starting in September 2026 and over a period of 18 months, the EFG Graduate Programme offers a challenging and exciting opportunity for recent graduates. Tailored on individual skills, interests, and competencies, the programme provides an ideal opportunity to obtain wide-ranging experience in an international and fast-growing private banking group:

  • You will start with a 2-week onboarding in Switzerland to discover EFG Mission, Vision and Culture and start building your professional network.
  • During the onboarding you will have the chance to attend specific trainings aimed at enhancing key competencies and communication skills.
  • Continuous learning opportunities to ensure you develop both personally and professionally to achieve full potential.
  • Buddy and Mentor to accompany you throughout the programme.
  • Truly global, we have positions based in Miami, London, Luxembourg, Geneva, Zurich, Lugano, Singapore and Hong Kong.

Could you be one of our future graduates?

We value diversity and we do not require specific academic majors or industry experience, but look for different mix of background, experiences and skills.

Our graduates are brilliant and enthusiastic talents, so we expect:

  • Bachelor or Master degree (completed in 2025 or on track to complete it) with outstanding academic results in a field with affinity with the selected professional path.
  • Excellent English (any other language will be an advantage)
  • International mindset
  • Strong analytical, problem solving and organisational skills
  • Forward thinking and able to suggest new ways of working to improve efficiency and competitiveness
  • Self-disciplined, committed and with strong work ethics
  • Adaptable and able to work collaboratively in a team environment with people from different backgrounds
  • Client-oriented
  • Proactive self-starter with intellectual curiosity

What will you get?

  • Dynamic work experience by rotating through different teams and business units
  • Experience an enriching international network
  • Opportunity to build expertise in your chosen field and contribute to the EFG Culture
  • Continuous development and learning opportunities on both technical and soft skills

The recruitment process for this position will begin in April 2026 and will include several interviews.

General Info

  • Work time Percentage: 100%

  • Location: St Moritz

Our Company

EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. Joining our team means experiencing a supportive environment, where your contributions are valued and recognised. We strongly believe that the diversity of our teams gives us a competitive advantage by fostering better decision-making and greater innovation.

Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.

Job Description

To support our growing activities in St. Moritz, we are looking for a Client Service Officer who will provide high-quality administrative and client service support to our Client Relationship Officers (CROs), while also contributing to the smooth running of our front office reception and cashier operations.

This role is ideal for a service-oriented, hands-on professional who enjoys working in a dynamic, international private banking environment and who is motivated to take ownership, act proactively and deliver an outstanding client experience.

Main Responsibilities

1. Client and CRO Support

  • Support clients and CROs in their daily business activities.

  • Maintain active client contact in written and spoken German and English.

  • Provide top-quality service to clients through regular and prompt contact by phone and email.

  • Execute client account openings, closures and updates, including:

    • Conducting internet searches and basic due diligence checks.

    • Collecting and verifying corroborative documentation.

  • Prepare and follow up on client visits, including:

    • Preparing account documentation and reviewing documents.

    • Organising account statements and other supporting material.

  • Execute and control client instructions, such as:

    • Stock market transactions.

    • Payments and transfers.

    • Foreign currency trades.

  • Perform various administrative and organisational tasks, including:

    • Client reviews and periodic checks.

    • Follow-up on missing or pending documents.

    • Delivery and monitoring of hold mail.

  • Prepare marketing and client materials, such as:

    • Presentations and pitchbooks.

    • Investment proposals & portfolio reviews.

  • Actively promote paperless accounts and e-banking usage to clients.

  • Ensure full compliance with the Code of Conduct and all internal regulations (e.g. Cross Border, AML, Clear & Clean Desk Policy, Absence Management).

2. Reception and Front Office Support

  • Welcome and assist clients upon arrival, ensuring a professional and warm first impression.

  • Manage reception duties, including:

    • Answering and directing phone calls.

    • Handling appointments and coordinating meeting room bookings.

    • Prepare and serve beverages to clients and ensure a high standard of hospitality.

    • Ensure meeting rooms and client areas are tidy, well-presented and adequately equipped at all times.

    • Handle incoming and outgoing mail and deliveries, ensuring timely distribution and proper documentation.

3. Cashier and Branch Support

  • Support daily cashier operations, including:

  • Processing client deposits and withdrawals.

  • Handling basic cash and payment transactions in line with internal guidelines.

  • Collaborate closely with CROs and other team members to ensure smooth branch operations.

  • Support marketing and client events, including preparation, logistics and on-site assistance.

Skills and experience

  1. Education and Professional Background

  • Commercial apprenticeship in a bank or similar banking-related education.

  • Further education in the financial sector is an advantage.

  • Minimum 3 years of experience in a private banking or intermediary environment.

  1. Skills

  • Good understanding of financial instruments, including:

  • Equities, bonds, structured products and derivatives.

  • Solid knowledge of Compliance and AML topics relevant to private banking.

  • Confident user of Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Word).

  • Familiarity with banking systems and front-office tools is an asset.

  • Strong client service orientation with excellent interpersonal skills.

  • High level of reliability, discretion and integrity.

  • Ability to cope with pressure and tight deadlines, with a flexible approach to working hours when required.

  • Comfortable working in a small branch environment, taking on a broad range of tasks (from client administration to reception and cashier support).

  • Proficient in written and spoken German and English (essential).

  • Italian is a strong plus; any additional languages are an advantage.

Our Values

  • Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

  • Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

  • Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence

  • Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

  • Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

  1. Please ensure to attach a cover letter to your CV when filling the application.

Application

  1. Should you wish to apply for this position use this link to apply.

summary: The International Payroll Specialist will be a key resource within an evolving global payroll structure. The role ensures accurate salary processing, full compliance with local and international regulations, and effective collaboration with internal and external partners to guarantee efficient and compliant payroll operations.

This position will primarily focus on EFG locations outside Switzerland and will serve as the main point of contact for payroll‑related matters across these locations. At the same time, the role will also be involved in payroll operations for Switzerland, providing support and backup to the team as needed. This role will provide guidance on payroll topics, including allowances, tax regulations, social security and reporting requirements, supporting the organisation in maintaining high standards of compliance and service quality.

The role also offers the opportunity to contribute to the implementation of a global payroll platform for employees of EFG international locations and to progressively take ownership of multi‑country payroll operations from Switzerland
responsibilities: Manage the global payroll process from Switzerland for selected international locations, ensuring accurate and timely salary payments.
Support and act as backup for payroll activities for Switzerland‑based employees.
Coordinate with local payroll providers, ensuring compliance with tax, social security and labour regulations in each jurisdiction.
Collaborate with HR and Finance to align processes, ensure accurate interfaces and resolve issues.
Act as first point of contact for employee payroll queries, providing timely and accurate support and maintaining high data quality across locations.
Prepare and review payroll reports (salaries, contributions, tax obligations) and support internal and external audits with required documentation.
Drive initiatives to review, streamline and centralise HR/payroll processes globally, performing regular checks on payroll outputs to ensure quality and compliance.
requirements: Proven experience in multi‑country payroll management, ideally within an international or financial services environment.
Strong knowledge of international payroll processes and compliance requirements (tax, social security, labour law) across multiple jurisdictions.
Experience with payroll platforms and HR systems, with strong attention to data quality and process reliability.
Structured, detail‑oriented and highly reliable, with strong organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple deadlines in a dynamic and demanding environment.
Comfortable working in a regulated and international context, with high levels of integrity and discretion in handling confidential data.
International mindset and experience working in multicultural environments.
Excellent interpersonal and collaboration skills, with a proactive and positive attitude, energy and intellectual curiosity.
Strong communication skills, with the ability to provide clear explanations to non‑specialist stakeholders.
Fluent in English; knowledge of national languages, particularly French and Italian, is a strong asset.

summary: The position of Operational Risk Analyst sits within the Operational Risk function and focuses on strengthening the bank’s risk management and internal control framework globally. Acting as a key risk partner to the first line of defence, the position provides independent challenge and oversight on operational risks, ensuring that processes, systems and activities are managed in line with the bank’s risk appetite.
Location: Lugano, Zurich, Geneva.
Main responsibilities
responsibilities: Oversee the effective and consistent implementation and maintenance of the risk and control management framework globally.

Contribute to thematic risk and control assessments on a broad range of issues, including back and middle-office, funds, securities, payments, corporate actions, and order execution through collaboration with other stakeholders.
Perform process-level walkthroughs and control testing to enable the identification and assessment of risks and controls.

Proactively escalate and effectively communicate material control weaknesses, closely collaborate with key stakeholders in the relevant functions to define appropriate action plans and oversee their timely implementation.
Provide expertise and make recommendations for improvements to steer adherence to internal regulations, regulatory requirements and industry best practices.
Master’s degree in Business Administration, Economics, or a technical field (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics).

At least 5 to 7 years of experience in the banking sector (ideally in Risk Control, Audit, or Business Risk Management), or in consulting/audit with a leading professional services firm; a combination of both is considered an advantage.
Strong understanding of banking processes and systems, products, and related regulatory requirements. Expertise with core banking systems and Front systems is a plus.
Excellent communication skills in English, both verbal and written. Knowledge of German, French, or Italian is a plus.
Proven ability to write reports and communicate effectively with a senior audience.
Self-motivated, diligent, highly organized, able to prioritize and multi-task, and thrive in a fast-paced environment.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, able to work autonomously and as part of a global team.
Innovative mindset, with the ability to identify opportunities for process improvement and operational efficiency.

summary: The IT Treasury and Trading team delivers efficient, effective support and IT solutions for applications and processes in client order execution, trading, treasury, and lending/collateral management. The team comprises both functional and technical experts supporting a Trading/Treasury room of approximately 50 traders. This role ensures continuity in client services and drives ongoing improvements in deliverable quality.
responsibilities: Run the Bank: end-to-end support on the IT trading platforms used for Securities Lending & Collateral, Islamic Finance, Securities and Derivatives execution and trading, spanning from application maintenance to analysis and resolution on daily incidents, liaising with external vendors where applicable.

Run the Bank: act as entry point to trading desk for broader issues that require liaising with other IT departments to cover the full stack (core banking and other linked applications, IT infa server, database, network, …) or with Operations teams (Securities, Derivatives, FX back office, middle office)

Change the Bank: coordinate and execute various IT projects aimed at evolving or upgrading the IT trading platforms, either developed and released internally or by application providers: collection of business needs, solution design and documentation, development where applicable and/or vendor coordination, creation of test cases catalogue and execution of IT tests, support to user acceptance test, rollout and post production support
Change the Bank: development of departmental applications aimed at covering gaps of the existing IT applications, mainly in the area of system integration and reporting
Change the Bank: management of tools for the automation of health checks on trading IT platforms to eventually guarantee the service to internal clients

updatedJobs.title: Apprendistato Impiegato/a di commercio AFC Banca

summary:
Stai finendo la scuole media e sei interessato a diventare un professionista in banca?

Se questo è il tuo desiderio l'Apprendistato di Commercio AFC ramo Banca di EFG Bank è quello che fa per te.

Lavorerai in delle sedi principali svizzere di EFG Bank in centro a Lugano da agosto 2026 fino a agosto 2029 quando avrai ottenuto l'Attestato Federale di Capacità.

Durante i tre anni farai esperienze in team di lavoro diversi che operano a livello nazionale e internazionale e si occupano ad esempio di Private Banking, Compliance, Credit Cards, Central Filing, Credit, Business Risk Management, Asset Management, etc.

Oltre alla formazione in azienda, in cui sarai seguito da un tutor a te dedicato in ogni team, frequenterai la scuola professionale commerciale (CPC) di tuo interesse e i corsi presso il centro competenze bancarie (CYP) assieme a tutti gli apprendisti di commercio ramo banca del Cantone.

EFG Bank supporta e incoraggia lo svolgimento della maturità professionale integrata per garantire una formazione il più possible completa al termine dell'apprendistato.

Durante il percorso potrai sviluppare diverse competenze professionali, tra le quali:

  • analisi del mercato finanziario e del credito
  • conoscenza dei prodotti bancari
  • elaborazione e verifica di documenti
  • pianificazione delle scadenze e gestione delle priorità
  • utilizzo dei sistemi informatici finanziari

Requisiti:

  • Passione per l’economia e il mondo bancario
  • Licenza di scuola media con ottimi risultati
  • Spiccate capacità relazionali e facilità ad adattarsi a diversi scenari lavorativi
  • Buone conoscenze informatiche
  • Conoscenza della lingua inglese

Se sei motivato, grazie per allegare alla candidatura online: una lettera di motivazione, il CV, le tre più recenti pagelle scolastiche e la licenza di scuola media.
The Talent Acquisition (TA) team is part of the Human Resources function and is responsible for attracting and hiring the best talent in the market for EFG Bank.

We are looking for a motivated Talent Acquisition Intern to join our team for a 6‑month internship in Lugano or Geneva. In this role, you will play a key part in enhancing the candidate experience, supporting Talent Acquisition in day‑to‑day activities, and contributing to reporting and process optimisation. You will also have the opportunity to support, and progressively manage, some junior recruitment mandates under guidance.
Key Responsibilities
responsibilities: Act as a point of contact for candidates, ensuring clear, timely and professional communication throughout the recruitment process.
Coordinate and schedule interviews with hiring managers and other stakeholders.
Support Talent Acquisition Specialists in screening CVs and assessing candidates against role requirements.
Draft and post job advertisements on internal and external platforms, ensuring consistency with EFG’s employer brand.
Execute daily operations in the Applicant Tracking System (ATS), including data entry, data quality checks and status updates.
Maintain accurate candidate records and ensure proper documentation of each recruitment step.
Prepare standard correspondence and communication to candidates and hiring managers.
Support coordination of assessments (tests, case studies, reference checks), where applicable.
Support the preparation of regular recruitment reports and simple dashboards (e.g. pipeline status, time‑to‑hire, sources of hire).
Help monitor key TA metrics and identify basic trends or bottlenecks in the process.
Contribute ideas to streamline and standardise recruitment processes, templates and communication flows.
Participate in small projects aimed at improving tools, candidate experience and internal TA efficiency.
Assist in sourcing candidates via LinkedIn Recruiter, job boards, referrals and other channels.
Conduct initial phone or video pre‑screenings for selected roles, under supervision.
Support employer branding initiatives (e.g. content preparation for social media or the career site, or job fairs).
Progressively support and, where appropriate, manage end‑to‑end recruitment for selected junior or intern positions, under the guidance of a Talent Acquisition Specialist.
requirements: Master’s degree (or currently enrolled in a Master’s program) in Human Resources, Business Administration, Psychology or a related field.
Previous experience (internship, part time role or similar) in recruiting, HR or employer branding is a plus.
Experience in data management, reporting or working with HR/ATS systems is an advantage.
Excellent command of English (spoken and written).

Interest in working with HR tools and platforms (e.g. Applicant Tracking Systems, LinkedIn Recruiter, career websites).

High attention to detail and accuracy in handling data and administrative tasks.
Strong interest in Talent Acquisition, the banking industry and investment management.

Proactive, curious and willing to take ownership of tasks and suggest improvements.
Team oriented, collaborative and comfortable working in an international and multicultural environment.

Job Description

The EPM OneStream Developer/Business Data Analyst will be part of the Group Performance Measurement, Budgeting & Reporting Team in Lugano and will contribute to develop and run an integrated planning, reporting and performance review process to facilitate value-driven business decision-making. In his role as EPM OneStream Developer, the resource will oversee running, maintenance and development of our existing OneStream planning platform. In his role as EPM OneStream Developer/Business Data Analyst, the resource will support the performance measurement activities by running granular analysis of financial performance root causes and by providing insights on value creation levers.

The resource will play an active role supporting business development and providing value-added decision support using data modelling and forecasting intelligence.

Main Responsibilities

  • EPM OneStream Developer
  • Business analyst and power user of MI data
  • Design and maintain data models for analysis and forecasting
  • Establish and compile dashboards and presentations to senior management

summary:
Our Company

EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

EFG Wealth Solutions is part of EFG International. EFG Wealth Solutions (Jersey) Limited ("EFGIJ"), together with its Jersey subsidiaries, are regulated by the Jersey Financial Services Commission and provide Trust Company Business, and Fund Services Business services to our client base.

EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. Joining our team means experiencing a supportive environment, where your contributions are valued and recognised. We strongly believe that the diversity of our teams gives us a competitive advantage by fostering better decision-making and greater innovation.

Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value - today and for the future.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.

Purpose of Position

To assume the role of Head of Trust Services, Jersey (the “Division”). This is a designated “senior management function” role as defined within the Financial Services Commission (Jersey) Law 1998 and you will discharge the obligations under this regime and in respect of a principal person satisfactorily and in line with defined business activities.

The Head of Trust Services, Jersey is responsible for driving organisation success by setting the strategic direction, having governance oversight, ensuring operational alignment and delivering results that support long term growth and sustainability.

Specifically, you will be directly responsible for leading and developing the Division by setting and implementing strategy for the growth of the business and driving forward the vision, mission, and values of EFG. To exercise accountability to the Board, Global Head of Trust Services, and relevant stakeholders and apply skill and care in exercising your duties, whilst ensuring operation and fiduciary risk exposures are managed to the highest standards and meeting financial performance objectives in line with financial plans.

To drive forward continuous growth in the business, generating meaningful and substantial business, revenue, and profitability growth in line with agreed budget and targets.

As a member of the executive board of EFGIJ, the role holder will drive business performance, foster a culture of excellence and integrity, and contribute to the broader strategic development of EFGIJ.

The position serves as a key liaison with regulators, clients and other stakeholders, ensuring that EFGIJ maintains its reputation as a trusted and leading fiduciary services provider.

Principal Responsibilities

Strategy

  • Contribute to the overall strategy, structure and prosperity of your Division. Actively participate in developing, implementing, and communicating the Division's agreed business plan/strategy. Contribute to the management of the local EFG business by acting as a director on the EFG in-house regulated companies, subsidiaries and as a committee member of in-house committees as required.
  • Lead, direct and manage the Division to ensure that it meets its regulatory, risk, financial and other stated objectives whilst also promoting and supporting the aims of EFG. Have the ability to strategically plan and proactively direct the Division and workloads to meet the needs of the business. Demonstrate and be recognised as the highest authority, delivering technical excellence in your specialist area and expertise in other relevant business and operational areas.
  • Focus on the development, maintenance, and growth of the Division, ensuring the delivery of high-quality fiduciary services, consistency, and operational efficiency in full compliance with regulatory obligations and internal risk frameworks.
  • Develop high level strategies for growth, efficiency, and competitive advantage. Demonstrate the ability to simultaneously be entrepreneurial, generating innovative ideas that are strategically sound and drive the company forward, maintaining a focus on the commercial needs, while keeping it under prudent control and acting responsibly towards employees, clients, and intermediaries.

Leadership

  • Accountable for the administration, operations, and accounting function within the Division, lead, provide support, encourage innovation, and motivate the management team in their day-today management of the wider administration, operations, and accounting teams. Monitoring of quality and accurate administration and output. Balance operational management and strategic initiatives.
  • Focus on leadership development, succession planning, and cultivating a high-performance culture. Build commitment with the administration, operations and accounting teams through fair, consistent, and persuasive behaviour, championing ideas and contributing to the setting of realistic yet challenging objectives through appraisal and other performance measurement initiatives.
  • Oversee resourcing, retention, and development in your Division. Develop commitment from your team and other colleagues through recognising good performance, providing timely and constructive feedback and through leading by example. Demonstrate the ability to lead and direct the efforts of others to achieve results.
  • Provide support to the Managing Director (Global Head of Trust Services) on any ad-hoc duties as required from time to time.

Relationship Management

  • Work collaboratively as part of the senior management team to support, cascade and embed business decisions, always behaving with the utmost integrity and professionalism.
  • Develop and maintain effective working relationships with all stakeholders. Play a key role in cross-functional collaboration. Communicate clearly and effectively, using authority and influence appropriately.
  • Inspire confidence in new and existing clients by working with key decision makers and influencers to build strong and effective relationships. Build, maintain and deepen strong relationships and credibility with colleagues, clients, and intermediaries by being approachable and proactive in sharing knowledge. Encourage teambuilding, collaboration, and a positive work culture.

Business Development

  • Drive forward and support business growth by the development of new business strategic plans and work with colleagues to attract opportunities and onboard new business from wider Group, external introductions, and prospective clients, supported by travelling to clients, and intermediaries as required. Act as one of the primary points of contact for new business enquiries, to include overseeing new business proposals and pitch documentation for prospective clients and/or intermediaries together with the senior management team.
  • Drive the achievement of the Division's budget and new business targets through proactive onboarding of new client relationships and the ongoing management and development of existing client relationships.
  • Maintain an awareness of the wider Business' and Division's service capabilities and create strategic alliances with third parties. Be a role model and ambassador for EFG both internally and externally, attending conferences, networking events and other relevant opportunities.
  • Maintain a knowledge of the external business environment and relevant industry specific trends, both locally and in other jurisdictions in which we have exposure, so that the range of services offered to clients is continually reviewed and to provide sound and practical advice both to colleagues and clients.

Financial

  • To drive forward change across the Division, which can be directly linked to increased profitability. Develop cost efficiencies through the introduction of new procedures, systems, working practices, improved financial management and other initiatives.
  • Lead commercial reviews of specific areas of the Division and provide reviews of findings and suggested action plans to the CEO EFGIJ.
  • Provide input to Divisional budgets and ensure financial results are monitored and continually compared with targets and activities are adjusted to ensure targets are met.

Operational

  • Attend, constructively contribute and chair internal committees/meetings as required from time to time and ensuring the timely and accurate reporting of key information where relevant. Act as a director/trustee of client entities as required.
  • Act as a point of contact for the administration, operations and accounting teams on technical and other matters within the Division. Demonstrate excellent knowledge of corporate governance requirements, internal compliance, anti-money laundering and other relevant policies and procedures.
  • Promote highest standards of client service administration and fiduciary services activities, ensuring that appropriate oversight is maintained thereby allowing fiduciary and operational risk to be effectively managed and controlled.
  • Work with the senior management team to identify areas to improve the client experience and implement operational efficiencies, ensuring that the Division is operating efficiently and effectively as possible. Contribute to and maintain adequate policies and procedures for the work conducted by the Division ensuring that these are aligned to EFG expected documentation standards.
  • Oversee resourcing, retention, and development in your function/team.
  • Act as an “A” Authorised Signatory
  • Take responsibility for own personal development, in line with agreed annual performance objectives.

Compliance & Risk

  • Contribute to and oversee the implementation of effective risk management frameworks.
  • Ensure clients are administered in line with EFG's risk management framework, structure specific and fiduciary risks are mitigated as appropriate. Comply with all local regulatory requirements including, where appropriate, laws, orders, codes of practice and guidance, Group and local policies and procedures relevant to the Business.
  • Ensure that all CPD requirements are met year on year for both you and all members of your team, and that training and development is relevant.

Qualifications, skills and experience

  • Recognised Professional Qualification - e.g. Accountancy, Chartered Secretary, STEP, Banking, Securities, Compliance or other relevant Table 4 qualification along with relevant experience as defined by the Jersey Financial Services Commission
  • 8 years' relevant finance industry experience working within an offshore financial services organisation in a similar role, with a strong technical understanding of all areas of client administration and fiduciary services.
  • Focussing on the development, maintenance, and growth of the [Division/Function] business, ensure quality levels of client service, consistency and operational efficiency. Ability to manage complexity and lead large-scale initiatives.
  • Comprehensive and detailed understanding of company law, current industry legislation, regulation and working practices and having due regard to corporate governance, compliance, and risk management aspects.
  • A good understanding of company financial management.
  • Experience of managing and leading people, driving a high-performance culture. Ability to communicate with other areas of the business and key stakeholders in a confident and professional manner.
  • Strong leadership and interpersonal skills; approachable and accessible with the ability to inspire, motivate and develop others, and manage underperformance quickly and fairly.
  • Proficient in the use of a range of Microsoft products (Outlook, Word, Excel and PowerPoint).

Our Values

  • Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement
  • Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results
  • Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence
  • Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset
  • Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.

Job Description

In the framework of the First Line of Defense (FLoD), Business Risk Management (BRM) ensures the effectiveness of the Internal Control System in the PB area. Under the supervision of the PB COO, BRM organizes and performs a certain number of tasks around the daily support & monitoring activities of the department. She/He coordinates the PB risk assessment in close alignment with Private Banking Support to achieve a robust FloD.

BRM focuses on ex-post controls, supports the Global BRM in the coordination of FLoD risk management activities, improves the quality of FLoD activity by ensuring that rules and processes are adhered to effectively, supports local CROs with the execution of their controls and acts as interface between the Business Head and 2nd and 3rd LoD functions.

1. Controlling

Execute all ex-post controls with focus on internal processes and regulatory topics, in line with PB control blueprint. Follow-up/resolution of operational alerts.

Perform additional / ad-hoc reviews as needed.

2. Reporting

Report on daily/monthly/quarterly controls and follow-up of detected issues.

Report on the internal control environment and potentially arising risks or issues.

Support production of CRO Risk Scorecards Perform additional / ad-hoc reporting as needed.

3. Reviewing

Support in enhancing effectiveness and efficiency of PB control and risk management activities.

Support PB risk assessments in coordination with the Second Line of Defence.

4. Miscellaneous

Participate in various front office, operational and risk management projects as requested.

Skills and experience

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Economics or Business Administration
  • At least 2 years of experience in the banking, insurance, or financial services sector in client/customer services
  • Good knowledge of Financial Products and ideally Private Banking
  • Advanced Excel skills

Our Values

Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement

Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results

Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence

Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset

Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

• Coordinate and facilitate trainings (system, process, policies/procedures) for Front Office and business support staff, ensuring all team members are up to date with the latest systems, operational processes, and policy/regulatory requirements. Maintain accurate attendance records and follow up with staff to ensure mandatory training is completed within required timeframes. Prepare and submit monthly training completion reports to PBCOO and HR.

• Manage and oversee onboarding training for new Front Office hires by tracking their completion of mandatory trainings. Liaise with relevant departments to ensure timely granting of system access rights upon successful completion of onboarding requirements, supporting a smooth transition into their roles.

• Organize monthly CRO/CSO meetings and collaborate with Department Heads to schedule and coordinate monthly meetings for Front Office. Prepare meeting materials/training deck and ensure that Front Office staff are informed of any updates or changes to policies, procedures, regulations, or systems that impact Private Banking operations.

• Partner with Market Group Heads on CRO/CSO cost allocation reporting to collect, review, and consolidate data for monthly CRO/CSO cost allocation reports. Ensure accuracy and timely submission of reports, supporting effective financial management and transparency within the Private Banking division.

• Handle and respond to ad-hoc requests for customized statements, reference letters, and perform reviews of operational incidents. Ensure all requests from Front Office are handled efficiently, accurately, and in accordance with internal policies and regulatory requirements.

• Provide prompt and professional support to Front Office staff and serve as a primary contact point for non-KYC related queries received via the PBS group mailbox. Deliver high-quality, timely support to enable Front Office staff to operate efficiently and effectively.

• Perform ex-ante compliance reviews of deposits initiated by Front Office staff, verifying compliance with regulatory requirements and internal guidelines. Ensure all checks are completed before daily cut-off times to mitigate operational and compliance risks.

• Participate in and contribute to special assignments, process improvement initiatives, and system enhancement projects as designated by Management. Where the PBCOO function serves as process owner, leads and manage change initiatives to optimize workflows and enhance system functionality.

• Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) for system changes and participate in UAT activities for new system implementations or enhancements. Organize the nomination of Front Office testers, provide guidance on testing procedures, and ensure thorough documentation and feedback to support successful system rollouts.

• Manage and oversee a range of administrative duties, such as distributing faxes, reassigning name screening tasks, and translating documents as needed. Ensure all administrative processes are handled efficiently to support the smooth operation of Private Banking activities.

• Monitor and manage the Client Enquiry and Feedback mailbox, ensuring all general client inquiries are acknowledged and responded to promptly and professionally. Escalate complex issues as necessary and maintain high standards of client service.

• Conduct data analysis and compile management information system (MIS) reports to support management reporting requirements. Provide analytical insights and data support to various risk and governance committees, facilitating informed decision-making within the Private Banking division.

• Support corporate governance initiatives and assist in the implementation and monitoring of corporate governance frameworks and practices within Private Banking. Ensure adherence to internal policies, regulatory requirements, and industry best practices. Contribute to the preparation of materials for governance committees and facilitate communication between stakeholders to promote accountability and transparency.

Requirements:

• University degree with a minimum of 8 years’ experience in financial services, preferable within Private Banking. Keen interest in the financial industry and wealth management.

• Strong understanding of relevant regulatory and compliance frameworks and comprehensive knowledge of banking operations

• Excellent command of both written and spoken English and Chinese (Cantonese & Putonghua)

• Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, including Outlook, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel.

• Experience in coordinating cross-functional projects or initiatives within a financial institution is an advantage, including process improvement, change management, or system implementation projects

• Collaborative team player with the ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment.

unchangedJobs.title: Business Risk Manager - Authorized Officer/Associate Director

summary:
Main Duties:

  • Accountable for the development of a comprehensive risk and control assessment for all aspects of risk within the FLoD, including the effective oversight of the testing programme, and review this with the business on a regular basis.

  • Responsible for ensuring that a comprehensive risk based control monitoring programme exists across the function, evidencing the adequacy and effectiveness of key controls

  • Perform regular business controls and monitoring on various areas, i.e. Investment Suitability, Cross Border, Anti-Fraud, etc., and produce management reporting on the findings.

  • Conduct thematic reviews, investigations and gap analysis on different aspects within the FLoD.

  • Participate in ad-hoc projects and work closely with stakeholders to implement changes & enhancements.

  • Responsible for the development of a strong risk awareness and client fairness culture across all aspects of the business.

  • Collaborate with internal stakeholders (i.e. Second Line of Defense (SLoD), Global Function) to identify, assess, and mitigate risks across business operations, and to ensure appropriate controls are in place.

  • Accountable for proactive escalation, reporting, provision of advice and challenging existing processes in relation to the business activities.

  • Undertakes any other duties within the Business Risk Management or as directed by the Head of Business Risk Management.
  • Manages FLOD Risk Scorecard and ensures proper tracking, execution and integrity of the score card.
  • Provides counsel to front office staff on investment advisory activities and to ensure compliance with in-house policies & procedures and local regulatory requirements.
  • Builds strong relationships and credibility with key internal stakeholders

Requirements:

  • Bachelor degree or above in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, or related fields
  • 6 years or above of experience in financial or banking industries
  • Prior experience with general risk management, including control framework is preferred
  • Understanding of products and Wealth regulatory banking environment
  • Experienced in liaising with internal business partners
  • Demonstrated ability to work with minimal supervision, multi-tasking and to prioritize tasks

Main Duties & Responsibilities:

  • Introduces the Bank and its banking services to prospective clients.
  • Provides support to Relationship Manager to achieve their business goals such as NNA / Revenue targets.
  • Markets and provides comprehensive private banking services to meet varied wealth management needs.
  • Performs KYC / AML due diligence on clients in compliance with the Bank’s general directive and ongoing periodic review from time to time.
  • Go through the Investment Risk Profile Questionnaire with clients at time of account opening as well as ongoing periodic review to ensure and update clients’ investment product suitability, risk tolerance and investment experience from time to time.
  • Liaises and coordinates with the Bank’s investment units, e.g. Treasury Department and Global Product Unit for passing on product information and markets commentary to clients from time to time.
  • Closely monitor clients’ portfolio and account activities to ensure the compliance with internal guidelines as well as external regulations from time to time. Monitor clients’ position to ensure collateral sufficiency at all times and prompt regularization of excesses, if any.
  • Prepares and maintains proper records including but not limited to account documentation, CIP, IRPQ, credit application and relevant documents, trade transactions and call reports, etc.
  • Accept, confirm and execute clients’ investment orders, including but not limited to money market, securities, investment funds and FX transactions. Confirm with / advise clients on their order status in a timely manner. From time to time go through the selling process, suitability check, key economic factors and main risk factors of certain products with clients when needed.
  • Liaises and coordinates with Credit Department to ensure all clients’ credit applications are properly processed.
  • Attends internal / external trainings / meetings when possible and required. Ensuring fit and proper requirements (such as CPT requirement, AML training, fraud training and compliance certification etc.) as stipulated by the regulatory are completely met.
  • General ad-hoc duties such as to perform routine follow up and administration work.
  • Assists to ad-hoc IT projects on enhancement or upgrade of current IT systems if required
  • Back up other Assistant Relationship Managers as and when required.

Qualifications:

  • University graduate
  • Preferably with 2-3 years of private banking experience
  • Fluent in English, Cantonese and Mandarin
  • Professional qualifications such as Enhanced Competency Framework (ECF) would be an advantage.
  • The employment is subject to Mandatory Reference Checking Scheme (MRCS) as per regulatory requirement. For details, please refer to Hong Kong Monetary Authority - Mandatory Reference Checking Scheme

Job Description

Introduction to the Team:

• DataOps Team is responsible for seemless integration, release and monitoring of mission critical data pipelines and workflows

• Currently based in Geneva, we are expanding our global footprint by establishing a virtual team presence in Singapore.

• Reporting to the local IT Head of Applications, the role will be a key pillar in our follow-the-sun support model, ensuring continuous operational excellence across Asia and Europe timezone.

• The team is responsible for building, testing, deploying and monitor the operating part of the platform.

• Take operational ownership of specific data services (change management, quality assurance, SLA, SOP)

• Track and ensure operations continuity of critical data pipelines across the group during Asian hours, adopting a follow-the sun support model.

• Design, implement, and optimize data pipeline in batch and streaming mode.

• Follow and apply CI/CD practice to automate and streamline deployments and integrations.

• Create, maintain, improve documentation and operational procedures as part of operational ownership

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to review governance and improve integration operations.

• Maintain high standards of data quality, reliability, and performance.

• Stay current with advancements in data engineering, of dataops tech stack, to support continuous improvement.

Requirements and Qualifications

• Experience in data engineering and data operations roles.

• Proven hands-on experience with SQL, Python, PySpark and Databricks

• Knowledge of micro-services architecture, API management and observability is highly desirable.

• Solid understanding of CI/CD platforms and processes (e.g., Azure DevOps, GitLabCI, GitHub Actions).

• Experience with cloud data platforms (Azure preferred).

• Demonstrated ability to produce rigorous, clear, and comprehensive documentation and procedures.

• Familiarity with database design.

• Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.

• Effective communication and collaboration skills.

Personal Attributes

• Responsible and reliable, especially during critical operational periods

• Rigorous and methodical in documentation and process creation

• Motivated and goal-oriented

• Curious, enthusiastic, creative, and proactive

• Passionate about analytics and technology, with a strong desire to learn and grow

• Fluent in spoken and written English

Job Description
EFG’s Information Security and BCM team, led by the Group CISO, sets and coordinates global information security strategy, initiatives, and standards across EFG International. The team conducts security risk assessments, manages vulnerability and threat programs, and owns security awareness and training. It safeguards EFG’s infrastructure, applications, and data against breaches, malware, third‑party risks, and cyber-attacks. Additionally, it strengthens the bank’s defence through robust Business Continuity Management, supporting operational resilience and coordinated incident response

The Information Security & BCM Head Asia reports to the Regional COO with a functional line to the Group CISO, and will (i) identify regional risks, threats, and vulnerabilities across the SG and HK branches; (ii) address gaps against local laws, regulations, and industry practices; (iii) develop and tailor information security controls to the region’s risks, adapt policies and procedures to local culture and laws, and facilitate Group strategic initiatives; and (iv) ensure adherence to global Business Continuity Management policies while adopting BCM best practices per local regulatory requirements.


Main responsibilities

Strategy, governance, and leadership

  • Support and implement EFG’s information security strategy and programs, ensuring alignment with local business objectives.

    • Provide regional leadership on information security across SG and HK; serve as the Singapore and Hong Kong Information Security Officer.

    • Manage information security programs directed by Head Office and act as the primary contact for regulatory inquiries, reviews, and inspections.

    • Stay current on regulatory changes and industry trends; perform gap analyses on regulatory papers, circulars, and directives, and recommend improvements.

Risk management, compliance, and audit

  • Identify, evaluate, and manage regional information security risks and vulnerabilities; ensure preventative actions close risk gaps.

    • Conduct regular security assessments and audits to ensure compliance with internal, regulatory, and industry requirements; ensure timely closure of audit items.

    • Develop and maintain local security policies, procedures, and standards aligned with Head Office and regulators.

    • Collaborate on Operational Resilience and Business Continuity (BCP); act as local BCM referant ensuring internal and external requirements (e.g., HKMA OR-2) are met.

Incident management and monitoring

  • Lead and coordinate incident response locally and regionally in close collaboration with the Head Office CISRT, including investigation, root cause analysis, and treatment plans.

    • Review data security breaches, implement remediation, and design and lead the annual cyber-security incident drill for SG & HK.

    • Collaborate with Group Information Security to deploy monitoring tools safeguarding bank information.

Access governance and secure operations

  • Oversee timely completion of local and regional user access recertifications.

    • Approve and periodically review privileged access for business and IT users; recertify IT administrator and technical account access on locally and regionally managed infrastructure.

    • Serve as gatekeeper for data extraction from secure zones.

Stakeholder engagement, communications, and training

  • Build and maintain relationships with stakeholders, clients, leadership, regulators, and Head Office teams.

    • Monitor and manage security communications from Head Office and regulators.

    • Lead security awareness and training programs for employees and relevant stakeholders.

Technology, assessments, and continuous improvement

  • Support assessments for infrastructure and applications; perform periodic security reviews and audits.

    • Recommend enhancements to controls and processes based on emerging threats and best practices.

Reporting, budgeting, and resource management

  • Report timely status of projects, programs, risks, events, and incidents to the Group CISO.

    • Prepare spending proposals; manage local security budgets and resources effectively.

Skills and experience
• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
• 10+ years in information security with strong knowledge of security principles, practices, and technologies.
